meaningful_threshold_basis: low-level alcohol exposure threshold for caution routing; current guidance emphasizes less alcohol is better and a US standard drink is 14 g alcohol
meaningful_threshold_source: CDC Alcohol Use and Your Health, https://www.cdc.gov/alcohol/about-alcohol-use/index.html; NIAAA standard drink definition, https://www.niaaa.nih.gov/alcohols-effects-health/alcohol-drinking-patterns; Dietary Guidelines for Americans 2025-2030 PDF, https://cdn.realfood.gov/DGA.pdf
supplement_threshold_basis: caution threshold; one standard-drink context for higher alcohol effects
supplement_threshold_source: Dietary Guidelines for Americans 2025-2030 current guidance via DietaryGuidelines.gov / RealFood.gov and HHS release, https://www.dietaryguidelines.gov/ and https://realfood.gov/; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 1 g/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1 g/serving and below 14 g/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 14 g/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Meaningful threshold is caution-only, not a beneficial target; 1 g separates trace/background alcohol from intentional or material recipe alcohol while 14 g remains standard-drink territory.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the 1.6 g/day adult male ALA adequate-intake reference used for omega-3 labeling context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Omega-3 Fatty Acids fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Omega3FattyAcids-HealthProfessional/; FDA omega-3 nutrient content claims final rule, https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2014/04/28/2014-09492/food-labeling-nutrient-content-claims-alpha-linolenic-acid-eicosapentaenoic-acid-and-docosahexaenoic
supplement_threshold_basis: common plant omega-3 supplement territory; use active ALA mass, not flaxseed-oil or seed mass
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS Omega-3 Fatty Acids fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Omega3FattyAcids-HealthProfessional/; FDA omega-3 nutrient content claims final rule, https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2014/04/28/2014-09492/food-labeling-nutrient-content-claims-alpha-linolenic-acid-eicosapentaenoic-acid-and-docosahexaenoic
trace_definition: < 0.16 g ALA/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 0.16 g ALA/serving and below 1000 mg ALA/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries.
supplement_definition: >= 1000 mg ALA/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Supplement threshold set to 1000 mg active ALA. Do not treat 1000 mg flaxseed oil as 1000 mg ALA; oil products must be converted by ALA content. Applies to ALA specifically, not EPA/DHA.

meaningful_threshold_basis: lowest human RCT dose identified for a measurable oral ARA effect on blood fatty-acid status; specific health-outcome benefits remain limited
meaningful_threshold_source: ARA systematic review of human RCTs, https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/british-journal-of-nutrition/article/systematic-review-of-the-effects-of-increasing-arachidonic-acid-intake-on-pufa-status-metabolism-and-healthrelated-outcomes-in-humans/6A0167CBF8EC148B4855C25D002E4AC4; adult ARA intake review, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6469145/; vegetarian-focused ARA review, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s12944-025-02645-z
supplement_threshold_basis: Not a typical consumer supplement target for this workflow; do not route supplement-level effects without ingredient- or product-specific arachidonic-acid evidence.
trace_definition: < 80 mg arachidonic acid/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 80 mg arachidonic acid/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: Not applicable for supplement-level routing unless future evidence supports a specific supplemental form and dose.
notes: Marked not typical for supplement routing. If arachidonic-acid bodybuilding or clinical supplement evidence is later added, create an effect-specific threshold in mg arachidonic acid/serving and review safety context.

meaningful_threshold_basis: specific saturated-fatty-acid caution router at a 1 g serving-level saturated-fat contribution; human behenic-acid data show cholesterol raising under controlled-diet conditions but do not establish a lower molecule-specific threshold
meaningful_threshold_source: Behenic acid human cholesterol study, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0002916523064456; WHO saturated fatty acid guideline PDF, https://iris.who.int/bitstreams/463fa93e-6c17-4e5b-a4d7-928354ea34c3/download; FDA saturated fat label context, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/interactivenutritionfactslabel/assets/InteractiveNFL_SaturatedFat_October2021.pdf
supplement_threshold_basis: Not a typical direct supplement target; route as dietary fatty-acid/caution context only when meaningful food-level dose and evidence support it.
trace_definition: < 1 g behenic acid/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1 g behenic acid/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: Not applicable for supplement-level routing unless future evidence supports a specific supplemental form and dose.
notes: Marked not typical for supplement routing. Do not infer supplement-level benefits for behenic acid from general fat or fatty-acid evidence.

meaningful_threshold_basis: dietary beta-carotene equivalent of 90 ug RAE, approximately 10% of the 900 ug RAE vitamin A Daily Value
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Vitamin A fact sheet with carotenoid RAE conversions, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminA-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: vitamin A context plus high-dose beta-carotene trial territory
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 1080 ug beta-carotene/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1080 ug beta-carotene/serving and below 15000 ug beta-carotene/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 15000 ug beta-carotene/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Changed from RAE-context units to beta-carotene mass to match the nutriment. 12 ug dietary beta-carotene approximates 1 ug RAE; high-dose supplemental beta-carotene has separate caution context.

meaningful_threshold_basis: FDA/Health Canada per-serving health-claim contribution toward about 3 g/day oat or barley beta-glucan for cholesterol/CHD context
meaningful_threshold_source: 21 CFR 101.81 soluble fiber health claim, https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-B/part-101/subpart-E/section-101.81; Health Canada oat beta-glucan claim assessment, https://www.canada.ca/en/health-canada/services/food-nutrition/food-labelling/health-claims/assessments/products-blood-cholesterol-lowering-summary-assessment-health-claim-about-products-blood-cholesterol-lowering.html; EFSA oat beta-glucan opinion, https://efsa.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.2903/j.efsa.2010.1885
supplement_threshold_basis: lower supplement-like serving threshold; cholesterol health-claim evidence usually targets about 3 g/day oat or barley beta-glucan
supplement_threshold_source: 21 CFR 101.81 soluble fiber health claim, https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-B/part-101/subpart-E/section-101.81; Health Canada oat beta-glucan claim assessment, https://www.canada.ca/en/health-canada/services/food-nutrition/food-labelling/health-claims/assessments/products-blood-cholesterol-lowering-summary-assessment-health-claim-about-products-blood-cholesterol-lowering.html; EFSA oat beta-glucan opinion, https://efsa.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.2903/j.efsa.2010.1885
trace_definition: < 0.75 g oat/barley beta-glucan/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 0.75 g oat/barley beta-glucan/serving and below 1 g oat/barley beta-glucan/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries.
supplement_definition: >= 1 g oat/barley beta-glucan/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Use 1 g as supplement-like routing, not as a full cholesterol-claim dose. Cholesterol/CHD effects should usually require about 3 g/day and eligible oat/barley beta-glucan context.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of 30 ug/day Daily Value and adult adequate-intake context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Biotin fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Biotin-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: 10% DV for meaningful; common high-dose supplement territory for supplement effects
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 3 ug/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 3 ug/serving and below 300 ug/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 300 ug/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Food-level adequacy threshold; do not use to support hair/nail claims unless evidence is deficiency-specific.

meaningful_threshold_basis: lowest consistent low-dose oral caffeine range identified for vigilance/alertness effects in human performance reviews; formal EFSA label-claim threshold remains higher at 75 mg
meaningful_threshold_source: caffeine cognitive-performance review, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0149763416300690; EFSA caffeine alertness opinion, https://efsa.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.2903/j.efsa.2014.3574; FDA caffeine consumer update, https://www.fda.gov/consumers/consumer-updates/spilling-beans-how-much-caffeine-too-much
supplement_threshold_basis: common perceptible caffeine dose; high single-dose caffeine context
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Consumer Update: Spilling the Beans: How Much Caffeine is Too Much?, https://www.fda.gov/consumers/consumer-updates/spilling-beans-how-much-caffeine-too-much; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 32 mg caffeine/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 32 mg caffeine/serving and below 200 mg/serving; eligible for caution/limit-oriented food-level health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 200 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Lowered from 40 mg for the stricter minimum-effect review. A human performance review reports vigilance benefits at small doses around 32 mg; EFSA health-claim substantiation for alertness remains 75 mg per serving, so stronger alertness claims should use that higher context.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of 1300 mg/day Daily Value; food-label significance and bone-health adequacy context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Calcium fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Calcium-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: 10% DV for meaningful; common supplemental calcium dose
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 130 mg/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 130 mg/serving and below 500 mg/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 500 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Retains 10% DV as food-level meaningful threshold; supplement-dose calcium remains a separate higher threshold.

meaningful_threshold_basis: one carbohydrate-counting serving with practical glycemic/energy relevance; total carbohydrate is not inherently beneficial
meaningful_threshold_source: CDC carbohydrate counting guidance, https://www.cdc.gov/diabetes/healthy-eating/carb-counting-manage-blood-sugar.html; FDA total carbohydrate Daily Value, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/interactivenutritionfactslabel/assets/InteractiveNFL_TotalCarbohydrate_October2021.pdf; FDA Nutrition Facts label percent-DV guidance, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/how-understand-and-use-nutrition-facts-label
supplement_threshold_basis: macronutrient energy contribution; sports-carbohydrate dose context
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 15 g total carbohydrate/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 15 g total carbohydrate/serving and below 30 g/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 30 g/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Raised from 5 g because 5 g is low/background for total carbohydrate; 15 g is a practical per-serving threshold for glycemic/energy routing.

meaningful_threshold_basis: food-level protein contribution aligned with 10% Daily Value-style protein routing; casein-specific satiety, muscle-protein, or overnight-protein claims generally require higher matched doses
meaningful_threshold_source: FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; International Society of Sports Nutrition protein position stand, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5477153/
supplement_threshold_basis: common casein/protein powder serving threshold; assumes user-provided ambiguous 20 g value refers to casein
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS Dietary Supplement Fact Sheet: Exercise and Athletic Performance, protein supplement context, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/ExerciseAndAthleticPerformance-HealthProfessional/; International Society of Sports Nutrition protein position stand, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5477153/
trace_definition: < 5 g casein protein/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 5 g casein protein/serving and below 20 g casein protein/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries.
supplement_definition: >= 20 g casein protein/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Assumed the ambiguous 20 g proposed value refers to Casein supplement threshold. Meaningful threshold lowered to 5 g as a food-level protein contribution; casein-specific clinical or performance claims should require matched higher dosing and matrix context. Casein allergy/intolerance routing is separate.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of 2300 mg/day Daily Value and electrolyte adequacy context
meaningful_threshold_source: FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; National Academies sodium/chloride intake release, https://www.nationalacademies.org/news/report-sets-dietary-intake-levels-for-water-salt-and-potassium-to-maintain-health-and-reduce-chronic-disease-risk; MedlinePlus chloride in diet, https://medlineplus.gov/ency/article/002417.htm
supplement_threshold_basis: 10% DV for meaningful; high electrolyte/salt context
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 230 mg/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 230 mg/serving and below 1000 mg/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 1000 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Usually arrives with sodium chloride; interpret alongside sodium/salt rows when the source compound is salt.

meaningful_threshold_basis: caution threshold at the boundary of FDA low-cholesterol labeling; dietary cholesterol should be kept low within healthy dietary patterns
meaningful_threshold_source: FDA cholesterol Nutrition Facts label PDF, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/interactivenutritionfactslabel/assets/InteractiveNFL_Cholesterol_October2021.pdf; AHA dietary cholesterol science advisory, https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/CIR.0000000000000743; Endotext diet and lipid effects, https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK570127/
supplement_threshold_basis: 10% caution context; high-cholesterol serving context
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 20 mg/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 20 mg/serving and below 150 mg/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 150 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Lowered from 30 mg to FDA low-claim boundary. Caution-only; dietary cholesterol effects are heterogeneous and often confounded with saturated fat.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of 550 mg/day Daily Value and adult adequate-intake context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Choline fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Choline-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: common choline supplement territory below the 550 mg adult Daily Value but clearly above ordinary trace food contribution
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS Choline fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Choline-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
trace_definition: < 55 mg/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 55 mg/serving and below 300 mg/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries.
supplement_definition: >= 300 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Set to 300 mg as a practical supplement-dose threshold. Pregnancy/lactation, liver, and neurodevelopment effects still require matched evidence context and total daily intake review.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of 35 ug/day Daily Value; evidence for glycemic effects is mixed, so use as adequacy/food-level routing threshold
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Chromium fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Chromium-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: 10% DV for meaningful; common chromium supplement trial dose
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 3.5 ug/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 3.5 ug/serving and below 200 ug/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 200 ug/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Do not infer diabetes or glucose-control effects from this threshold alone; matched human evidence is required.

meaningful_threshold_basis: lowest human dose identified with a reported vascular flow-mediated-dilation effect in cocoa-flavanol dose-response evidence; formal EFSA/FDA claim context remains 200 mg/day
meaningful_threshold_source: cocoa flavanol dose-response/FMD meta-analysis, https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlehtml/2019/fo/c9fo01747j; cocoa flavanol dose-response study summary noting 80 mg/day increased FMD, https://www.researchgate.net/publication/267930544_Cocoa_consumption_dose-dependently_improves_flow-mediated_dilation_and_arterial_stiffness_decreasing_blood_pressure_in_healthy_individuals; EFSA cocoa flavanols opinion, https://efsa.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.2903/j.efsa.2012.2809; FDA cocoa flavanol qualified-claim letter, https://www.fda.gov/media/165090/download
supplement_threshold_basis: human cocoa flavanol intervention dose context
supplement_threshold_source: No established FDA Daily Value found in FDA Daily Value reference table; leave amount blank for LLM/reviewer context; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 80 mg cocoa flavanols/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 80 mg cocoa flavanols/serving and below 500 mg cocoa flavanols/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 500 mg cocoa flavanols/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Lowered from 200 mg for minimum-effect routing. Use 80 mg only for low-threshold vascular/FMD context; EFSA-authorized and FDA qualified-claim contexts still use at least 200 mg/day, and cocoa/chocolate mass is not interchangeable without flavanol assay data.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of 0.9 mg/day Daily Value and adult RDA context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Copper fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Copper-HealthProfessional/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: 10% DV for meaningful; high dietary/supplement caution context
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 0.09 mg/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 0.09 mg/serving and below 2 mg/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 2 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Food-level adequacy threshold; high-dose copper has separate safety/caution context.

meaningful_threshold_basis: lower end of authoritative pregnancy guidance recommending an additional 100-200 mg/day DHA on top of general EPA+DHA intake; stronger birth-outcome trials often use higher doses
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Omega-3 Fatty Acids fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Omega3FattyAcids-HealthProfessional/; omega-3 pregnancy clinical practice guideline, https://www.ajogmfm.org/article/S2589-9333(23)00393-2/fulltext; DHA pregnancy systematic review, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0271531724000873
supplement_threshold_basis: common DHA supplement and pregnancy-guidance territory; 20 mg is too low for supplement-level routing
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS Omega-3 Fatty Acids fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Omega3FattyAcids-HealthProfessional/; EFSA long-chain omega-3 safety/intake context, https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/press/news/120727
trace_definition: < 100 mg DHA/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 100 mg DHA/serving and below 200 mg DHA/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries.
supplement_definition: >= 200 mg DHA/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Set to 200 mg DHA rather than 20 mg. For preterm-birth, infant-outcome, omega-3-index, or triglyceride claims, require effect-specific matched dosing because many trials use higher DHA or combined EPA+DHA doses.

meaningful_threshold_basis: lowest dose in a human dose-ranging study showing systemic erythritol absorption and erythronate formation; platelet-reactivity evidence used a higher 30 g acute dose
meaningful_threshold_source: FDA erythritol GRAS notice response, https://www.fda.gov/media/121983/download; FDA evaluation of erythritol article, https://www.fda.gov/media/182122/download; erythritol dose-ranging absorption/metabolism study, https://www.mdpi.com/1422-0067/23/17/9867; erythritol platelet-reactivity study, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39114916/
supplement_threshold_basis: high-exposure/caution threshold near adult tolerance and platelet-reactivity study exposure; not a beneficial supplement target
supplement_threshold_source: FDA GRAS Notice inventory for erythritol, https://www.fda.gov/food/generally-recognized-safe-gras/gras-notice-inventory; EFSA sweeteners/erythritol reassessment context, https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/topics/topic/sweeteners; erythritol platelet-reactivity study context, https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-023-02223-9
trace_definition: < 10 g erythritol/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 10 g erythritol/serving and below 30 g erythritol/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries.
supplement_definition: >= 30 g erythritol/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Set to 30 g as high-exposure/caution routing, not a supplement benefit dose. GI tolerance and cardiometabolic caution depend on bolus size, body weight, beverage/food matrix, and repeated exposure.

meaningful_threshold_basis: lower end of the 3-5 g fat per meal range reported to support carotenoid/fat-soluble compound absorption; total fat amount alone is not inherently beneficial
meaningful_threshold_source: dietary factors and carotenoid bioavailability review, https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022316622139428; beta-carotene absorption review, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12002680/; FDA total fat Daily Value PDF, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/interactivenutritionfactslabel/assets/InteractiveNFL_TotalFat_October2021.pdf
supplement_threshold_basis: macronutrient energy and fat-soluble absorption context
supplement_threshold_source: FDA Daily Value on Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels, content current 2024-03-05, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels; dose-band thresholds are draft Sipz review thresholds
trace_definition: < 3 g total fat/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 3 g total fat/serving and below 20 g/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 20 g/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Lowered from 4 g to the lower end of human absorption evidence. Use mainly for fat-soluble nutrient/carotenoid absorption or macronutrient context; fatty-acid composition still matters for health direction.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the 28 g/day dietary fiber Daily Value; FDA-recognized fiber effects include bowel movement frequency and glucose/cholesterol effects for qualifying fibers

meaningful_threshold_basis: lower long-chain omega-3 effect anchor from DHA intake-response evidence and pregnancy/fish-intake context; general adult cardiovascular intake targets remain 250-500 mg/day EPA+DHA

meaningful_threshold_basis: food acidity routing threshold; pH is not an oral nutrient amount and should not use percent-DV logic
meaningful_threshold_source: FDA acidified and low-acid foods guidance, https://www.fda.gov/food/guidance-documents-regulatory-information-topic-food-and-dietary-supplements/acidified-low-acid-canned-foods-guidance-documents-regulatory-information; 21 CFR Part 114 acidified foods, https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-21/chapter-I/subchapter-B/part-114; Penn State acidified-food regulatory overview, https://extension.psu.edu/acidified-and-low-acid-food-regulatory-requirements

notes: Non-dose property. Use only for acidity/food-safety or dental-erosion style routing; pH direction is inverse, so the meaningful definition uses pH <= 4.6.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the 700 mg/day adult RDA, used as the minimum adult adequacy contribution threshold; FDA label Daily Value remains higher at 1250 mg

meaningful_threshold_basis: sensitive-subject FODMAP/polyol routing threshold for sorbitol or mannitol-type polyols; general laxative or healthy-adult GI effects usually require much higher doses
meaningful_threshold_source: low-FODMAP polyol cutoff discussion, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9956220/; polyol GI systematic review, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5508768/; FDA sugar alcohol label PDF, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/interactivenutritionfactslabel/assets/InteractiveNFL_SugarAlcohols_October2021.pdf

notes: Lowered from 5 g for minimum-effect routing in IBS/FODMAP-sensitive contexts. Polyols differ widely: erythritol is usually better tolerated than sorbitol or mannitol, and healthy-adult laxative effects usually need much higher doses.

meaningful_threshold_basis: broad non-trace polyphenol exposure threshold; total polyphenols are heterogeneous and specific subclasses or assayed compounds have stronger dose anchors

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the lower adult linoleic-acid adequate intake, used as a broad minimum PUFA adequacy/replacement-context threshold
meaningful_threshold_source: FDA linoleic-acid nutrient content claim notification, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-food-labeling-and-critical-foods/nutrient-content-claims-notification-foods-and-dietary-supplements-containing-linoleic-acid; EFSA opinion on mono/polyunsaturated fat replacement and blood cholesterol, https://efsa.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.2903/j.efsa.2011.2168; AHA dietary fats presidential advisory, https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/cir.0000000000000510

trace_definition: < 1.5 ug/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1.5 ug/serving and below 25 ug/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 25 ug/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Food-level vitamin D routing should stay in calcium/bone and adequacy context; correcting deficiency or changing serum 25(OH)D usually requires individualized higher dosing.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the 15 ug/day adult vitamin D RDA applied to ergocalciferol; D2 raises vitamin D status, though D3 may raise it higher/longer
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Vitamin D fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminD-HealthProfessional/; NIH ODS Vitamin D consumer fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminD-Consumer/; MedlinePlus Vitamin D overview, https://medlineplus.gov/vitamind.html
supplement_threshold_basis: ergocalciferol/D2 supplement dose aligned to 1000 IU vitamin D routing threshold
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS vitamin D fact sheet: D2/ergocalciferol and D3/cholecalciferol are supplemental forms, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminD-HealthProfessional/; Kaiser Permanente ergocalciferol D2 1000 IU capsule page, https://healthy.kaiserpermanente.org/health-wellness/drug-encyclopedia/drug.ergocalciferol-vitamin-d2-1-000-unit-capsule.443466; DailyMed ergocalciferol label states 1 mcg D2 equals 40 IU, https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/lookup.cfm?setid=bc3ed1e0-0bb9-1cc0-e053-2995a90a48fe
trace_definition: < 1.5 ug vitamin D2/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1.5 ug vitamin D2/serving and below 25 ug vitamin D2/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 25 ug vitamin D2/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Use for ergocalciferol/plant or mushroom-derived vitamin D; do not infer D3-equivalent potency beyond vitamin D status context without dose-specific evidence.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the 15 ug/day adult vitamin D RDA applied to cholecalciferol
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Vitamin D fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminD-HealthProfessional/; NIH ODS Vitamin D consumer fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminD-Consumer/; MedlinePlus Vitamin D overview, https://medlineplus.gov/vitamind.html
supplement_threshold_basis: common 1000 IU cholecalciferol/D3 supplement dose
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS vitamin D fact sheet: D2 and D3 are main supplemental forms, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminD-HealthProfessional/; Nature Made vitamin D3 1000 IU / 25 mcg label, https://www.amazon.com/Nature-Made-Vitamin-1000-Softgels/dp/B004U3Y8OM; Nature Made D3 1000 IU product page, https://www.marianos.com/p/nature-made-vitamin-d3-1000-iu-25-mcg-tablets/0003160401870
trace_definition: < 1.5 ug vitamin D3/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1.5 ug vitamin D3/serving and below 25 ug vitamin D3/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 25 ug vitamin D3/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Use for cholecalciferol/animal or lichen-derived vitamin D3; higher-dose deficiency correction and serum 25(OH)D claims need separate evidence.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the 15 mg alpha-tocopherol/day FDA Daily Value and adult RDA context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Vitamin E fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminE-HealthProfessional/; NIH ODS Vitamin E consumer fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminE-Consumer/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: lower common standalone vitamin E supplement dose (100 IU natural vitamin E equivalent)
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS vitamin E fact sheet: most vitamin-E-only supplements provide at least 67 mg / 100 IU natural vitamin E, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminE-HealthProfessional/; Linus Pauling Institute conversion example: 100 IU natural vitamin E provides 67 mg alpha-tocopherol, https://lpi.oregonstate.edu/mic/vitamins/vitamin-E; Nature Made vitamin E 400 IU / 180 mg label, https://www.naturemade.com/products/nature-made-vitamin-e-180-mg-400-iu-dl-alpha-softgels
trace_definition: < 1.5 mg alpha-tocopherol/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 1.5 mg alpha-tocopherol/serving and below 67 mg alpha-tocopherol/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 67 mg alpha-tocopherol/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Use alpha-tocopherol units; high-dose antioxidant or bleeding-risk claims should not be inferred from food-level vitamin E.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the lower adult Adequate Intake: 90 ug/day for adult women; use as a food-level meaningful threshold for normal clotting and bone-metabolism context
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Vitamin K fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminK-HealthProfessional/; NIH ODS Vitamin K consumer fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminK-Consumer/; Harvard Nutrition Source Vitamin K overview, https://nutritionsource.hsph.harvard.edu/vitamin-k/
supplement_threshold_basis: common vitamin K2 supplement softgel dose; anticoagulant interaction caution
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS vitamin K fact sheet and warfarin consistency caution, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminK-HealthProfessional/; NIH ODS consumer fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/VitaminK-Consumer/; Nature Made vitamin K2 100 mcg label, https://www.naturemade.com/products/vitamin-k2-100-mcg-softgels
trace_definition: < 9 ug/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 9 ug/serving and below 100 ug/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 100 ug/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Vitamin K has medication interaction context with warfarin; food-level claims should emphasize normal clotting/bone metabolism and consistency of intake where relevant.

meaningful_threshold_basis: 10% of the lower adult RDA: 8 mg/day for adult women; zinc is required for enzyme activity immune function protein and DNA synthesis wound healing and cell signaling
meaningful_threshold_source: NIH ODS Zinc fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Zinc-HealthProfessional/; NIH ODS Zinc consumer fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Zinc-Consumer/; FDA Daily Values, https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels
supplement_threshold_basis: lower common standalone zinc supplement dose; below higher 30-50 mg products
supplement_threshold_source: NIH ODS zinc fact sheet, https://ods.od.nih.gov/factsheets/Zinc-HealthProfessional/; NIH DSLD Zinc Citrate 15 mg label, https://dsld.od.nih.gov/label/282724; Nature Made zinc 30 mg label, https://www.naturemade.com/products/zinc-30-mg-tablets
trace_definition: < 0.8 mg/serving; do not surface direct health-effect claims, only optional background presence language.
meaningful_definition: >= 0.8 mg/serving and below 15 mg/serving; eligible for normal food-level/naturally occurring health-effect summaries when evidence context supports the effect.
supplement_definition: >= 15 mg/serving; supplement-level, therapeutic-dose, high-caution, or high-exposure effects may be considered when the effect context matches the recipe dose.
notes: Food-level zinc routing should stay in normal immune/metabolic contribution context; high-dose zinc can interfere with copper and needs separate supplement-dose caution.
